commit.message:
feat: Redis LLM cache, CRM domain, expanded connectors (Phase 3 & 4)

Phase 3 — Redis LLM result cache:
- Add core/cache.py: transparent Redis-backed cache for LLM responses
  * dataset_fingerprint() — SHA-256 of first 1k rows (8-char hex key suffix)
  * make_cache_key(goal, df, model) — deterministic key: llm:<goal>:<model>:<fp>
  * cache_get / cache_set — lazy Redis client, graceful fallback on errors
  * cached_llm_call() — wraps any LLM callable; hits skip OpenAI entirely
  * Disabled automatically when REDIS_URL env var is absent (zero config)
  * TTL configurable via REDIS_CACHE_TTL_S env var (default 3600 s)
- Wire cached_llm_call into _call_llm in core/analysis.py; all 5 analysis
  methods now pass goal + df so repeat runs on unchanged data are instant
- Export cache functions from core/__init__.py
- tests/test_redis_cache.py: 18 tests (fingerprint, key generation,
  get/set with mocked Redis, cache hit/miss, TTL, none-not-cached,
  disabled-without-Redis) — all passing, no real Redis required

Phase 4 — CRM domain:
- Add prompts/crm_goals.yaml: 3 CRM goals with full prompt chains
    improve_lead_conversion: trend + anomaly + ranking + prediction
    reduce_churn:            trend + anomaly + ranking + prediction
    increase_upsell:         trend + ranking + prediction
- Register all 3 CRM goals in AnalysisPipeline.goal_chains
- Add _identify_metrics() entries for all 3 CRM goals (9 new KPIs)
- Expand _ingest_crm() stub with inline HubSpot and Salesforce REST API
  call patterns (mirrors the Shopify stub style)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(nvim): restructure config into modular lua files (#2)

Splits the monolithic `init.lua` into a clean modular structure.

## Structure

```
nvim/
├── init.lua                    # entry point — require only
└── lua/
    ├── config/
    │   ├── options.lua         # vim.opt settings
    │   ├── keymaps.lua         # key mappings (<Space> leader)
    │   └── lazy.lua            # lazy.nvim bootstrap
    └── plugins/
        └── colorscheme.lua     # gruvbox dark
```

## Changes

- `init.lua`: reduced to 3 require calls
- `options.lua`: line numbers, indent
(expandtab/smartindent/shiftwidth=2), search, scrolloff, clipboard,
persistent undo, etc.
- `keymaps.lua`: window navigation, visual indent, line move, clear
search highlight
- `lazy.lua`: lazy.nvim bootstrap extracted from init.lua, loads
`lua/plugins/` directory
- `plugins/colorscheme.lua`: gruvbox plugin spec extracted from init.lua

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Mar 5 shadow trades — all 5 signals blocked by triangulation gate

Scanned 9 NBA + 8 NHL games. 5 raw Elo signals found:
- DET YES @ SA (Elo 73.4% vs Poly 40.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 41.8% agrees with Poly
- GS YES @ HOU (Elo 32.6% vs Poly 23.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 24.7% agrees with Poly
- NO YES @ SAC (Elo 75.3% vs Poly 65.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 63.4% agrees with Poly
- CHI YES @ PHX (Elo 25.6% vs Poly 17.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 19.2% agrees with Poly
- NYI YES @ LA (Elo 53.5% vs Poly 45.5%) — BLOCKED: SB 43.1% agrees with Poly

Triangulation gate working: in all 5 cases, sportsbooks confirm
Polymarket pricing and Elo is the outlier. 0 paper trades placed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rebuild reactjs/ section: 33 topics with consistent 8-section format (#4)

* Initial plan

* Add ReactJS topics 01-08 with comprehensive interview-ready content

- 01-components.md: Functional vs Class, PureComponent, React.memo, HOCs, composition
- 02-jsx.md: JSX syntax, createElement, Fragments, conditional rendering patterns
- 03-props.md: Passing data, destructuring, defaultProps, PropTypes, children, compound components
- 04-state.md: useState, functional updates, immutability, batching, derived state
- 05-lifecycle-hooks.md: Class lifecycle methods, useEffect equivalents, comparison table
- 06-hooks-overview.md: All 12 built-in hooks with examples and when-to-use reference
- 07-rules-of-hooks.md: Call order internals, violations, ESLint enforcement
- 08-useEffect-useLayoutEffect.md: Timing differences, cleanup, race conditions, SSR safety

Each file follows the 8-section format with 260-420 lines of content including
basic/intermediate/advanced examples, pitfalls, and interview Q&A tables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReactJS topics 09-16: useEffect problems, useMemo/useCallback, useReducer, useRef, custom hooks, Context API, Redux/RTK, Flux

- 09: useEffect Problems & Solutions (infinite loops, missing deps, stale closures, race conditions, cleanup)
- 10: useMemo & useCallback (memoization, performance optimization, when to use)
- 11: useReducer (reducer pattern, complex state, action types, useContext integration)
- 12: useRef & useImperativeHandle (DOM refs, mutable values, forwardRef, custom ref APIs)
- 13: Custom Hooks (useFetch, useLocalStorage, useDebounce, useWindowSize, composition)
- 14: Context API (createContext, Provider, useContext, performance, multiple contexts)
- 15: Redux & Redux Toolkit (createSlice, configureStore, createAsyncThunk, RTK Query)
- 16: Flux Pattern & Architecture (unidirectional flow, Dispatcher, Stores, Flux vs Redux)

Each file: 8 required sections, 350-500+ lines, complete code examples, navigation links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReactJS topics 17-24: state management through controlled/uncontrolled components

- 17: State Management Patterns (local/global state, Context, Redux, Zustand, server state)
- 18: React.memo & Performance (memoization, useCallback, useMemo, profiling)
- 19: Code Splitting & Lazy Loading (React.lazy, Suspense, route-based splitting, prefetching)
- 20: Reconciliation & Virtual DOM (diffing algorithm, keys, React Fiber, commit phases)
- 21: React DOM Rendering (createRoot, render/commit phases, batching, Concurrent Mode)
- 22: Higher-Order Components (auth, logging, theming, HOC vs hooks comparison)
- 23: Render Props (children as function, data providers, Toggle, vs hooks)
- 24: Controlled vs Uncontrolled Components (React state vs DOM state, refs, form strategies)

Each file follows the 8-section format with 193-345 lines of comprehensive content
and complete, runnable JavaScript/JSX code examples.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ReactJS topics 25-33 and update README

- 25-forwarding-refs.md: React.forwardRef, useImperativeHandle, HOC pattern
- 26-error-boundaries.md: getDerivedStateFromError, componentDidCatch, react-error-boundary
- 27-react-router.md: v6 API, nested routes, protected routes, hooks
- 28-forms.md: controlled inputs, custom useForm hook, Formik, React Hook Form
- 29-dom-manipulation.md: querySelector, useRef, D3 integration, IntersectionObserver
- 30-appendChild-classList.md: DOM insertion methods, classList API, DocumentFragment
- 31-portals.md: createPortal, modals, tooltips, event bubbling behaviour
- 32-testing.md: Jest, RTL, userEvent, async testing, renderHook
- 33-data-fetching.md: fetch/axios, React Query, SWR, AbortController, race conditions
- README.md: updated with full 33-topic table of contents and study guide

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rebuild reactjs/ with 33 comprehensive topics in 8-section format + update main README

commit.message:
Fix test_no_optuna reinstalling optuna at wrong version (#1522)

test_no_optuna() uninstalls optuna to test graceful handling, then
reinstalls it. It was hardcoded to reinstall optuna==2.8.0 instead of
the version range used elsewhere (>=2.8.0,<=3.6.1), leaving the test
environment with a downgraded optuna for the rest of the CI run. This
caused the saved dependency snapshot to incorrectly report optuna==2.8.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(apple-silicon): deflake NEON RMSNorm proptests with bounded input range (#3337)

## Problem
NEON RMSNorm property tests fail on macOS ARM64 CI (`Build & Test (macOS
ARM64)`) with 4/16 failures:
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_unit_rms`
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_preserves_sign`
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_gamma_scaling`
- `prop_neon_rmsnorm_matches_scalar`

- Rejects 78% of generated cases (780/1000 = wasted test budget)
- Remaining cases near 1e18 still overflow f32 when squared across large
vectors

## Fix
Replace `prop::num::f32::NORMAL` with bounded `-100.0f32..100.0f32` in
all 4 RMSNorm tests. This matches the approach already used by the
LayerNorm property tests (`-10.0..10.0`) and is realistic for neural
network activation values.

## Testing
- 5 consecutive local runs: **22/22 pass, 0 failures** ✅
- No test budget wasted on unrealistic input ranges
- Zero prop_assume rejections

Labels: apple-silicon, test-fix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix tool calling with OpenAI Codex models (#1893)

* Fix tool calling with OpenAI Codex models

Schema generation fixes:
- Add additionalProperties: false to ToolInputSchema
- Map enum types to "string" with Enum.GetNames() values
- Add enum array property to ToolPropertySchema
- Omit empty descriptions instead of serializing as ""

Argument parsing fixes:
- Add camelCase fallback matching (strip hyphens) in ParseFromDictionary
- Add flat-argument fallback in GetParametersFromArgs

Other fixes:
- Fix typo "nad" -> "and" in ServerToolLoader and SingleProxyToolLoader
- Rewrite flaky VisualStudioToolNameTests to use in-process CommandFactory

New:
- Add ~40 tests covering all schema and parsing fixes
- Add eng/scripts/Preflight.ps1 CI preflight check script

Fixes #1892
Related: #1752, #1069
Related: modelcontextprotocol/csharp-sdk#342, modelcontextprotocol/csharp-sdk#460

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback

- Remove unused usings in VisualStudioToolNameTests
- Make 'parameters' key lookup case-insensitive in GetParametersFromArgs
- Fix shebang to #!/usr/bin/env pwsh in Preflight.ps1
- Update Preflight.ps1 help to list all 7 steps including AOT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: fix StorageSync recorded test with correct param names

The Should_Crud_storage_sync_service test was sending camelCase param
names (incomingTrafficPolicy, identityType) that didn't match the
kebab-case CLI options (--incoming-traffic-policy, --identity-type).
These params were silently dropped, making the update a no-op.

Changes:
- Fix param names to use kebab-case matching CLI options
- Add [CustomMatcher(compareBody: false)] temporarily since the
  recording was made with the no-op behavior
- Created #1895 to track re-recording with correct behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make metaKeys a static readonly field on the class

Address review feedback from alzimmermsft: extract the metaKeys
HashSet from GetParametersFromArgs into a private static readonly
field to avoid allocating a new HashSet on every method call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

## Summary
Fix clippy warnings across ~41 test files in `bitnet-kernels`, improving
code quality.

## Changes
- Replace manual `div_ceil` with `.div_ceil()`
- Replace manual `is_multiple_of` with `.is_multiple_of()`
- Use `std::f32::consts::PI` instead of approximate `3.14`
- Fix collapsible if statements
- Fix needless range loops
- Fix excessive float precision
- Fix no-effect operations

## Testing
- `cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo fmt --all --check` ✅

Make integration tests skip gracefully when models are missing

Skip.Unless() calls that use xUnit's dynamic skip protocol. Tests
now report as 'skipped' instead of falsely 'passed' when ONNX model
files are not present (e.g. in CI without downloaded models).

commit.message:
Improve performance: slices, partial-read pre-filter, larger I/O buffer

- Replace container/list with []string in SizeMap and DuplicateMap:
  reduces per-file heap allocations and improves cache locality
- Add FilterByPartialHash phase: reads only the first 4 KB of each
  candidate file before the full MD5 pass; files that differ in the
  first 4 KB cannot be duplicates, so they are skipped entirely.
  Dramatically reduces I/O for large files with many same-size peers.
- Use io.CopyBuffer with a 1 MB buffer (via sync.Pool) for full
  checksumming, reducing syscall overhead on large files
- Update verbose timing output to show the new partial-hash phase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make bootstrap_worktree self-init aware for wade development (#57)

* chore: scaffold branch for #55

* feat: make bootstrap_worktree self-init aware for wade development

When developing wade itself, detect self-init by comparing repo_root
to the wade package root. When self-init is detected, install skills
as symlinks pointing to the worktree's own templates/ directory so
template edits are immediately reflected.

- Add get_wade_repo_root() to installer.py (shared self-init detection)
- Add optional templates_dir parameter to install_skills()
- Update bootstrap_worktree to detect self-init and pass worktree templates
- DRY up init_service.py to use shared get_wade_repo_root()
- Add tests for self-init and non-self-init bootstrap behavior

Closes #55

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address all code review issues

- Locked log files no longer crash the script (ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)

commit.message:
test: add proptest-based fuzzing harnesses for all HID protocol crates (#164)

Add property-based test harnesses to ensure malformed HID reports
never cause panics, undefined behavior, or safety violations across
all supported device vendors.

Crates with new proptest harnesses:
- hid-fanatec-protocol: standard, extended, pedal parsers
- hid-simucube-protocol: HID report, input report, steering bounds
- hid-asetek-protocol: parse never panics, derived finite, truncation
- hid-cammus-protocol: parse never panics, axes bounded, truncation
- hid-openffboard-protocol: parse, steering, button safety, truncation
- hid-ffbeast-protocol: parse, parse_with_id, normalized, wrong ID
- hid-heusinkveld-protocol: parse, normalized finite, truncation
- hid-button-box-protocol: gamepad, extended, axis, button, truncation
- hid-moza-protocol: HBP, SRP, any PID, axes bounded
- hid-thrustmaster-protocol: enhanced with parse_input, parse_pedal,
  axes_bounded, truncation tests

Cross-vendor integration test:
- New hid_cross_vendor_fuzz_tests.rs feeds random bytes to all 14+
  parser functions simultaneously, including edge cases (empty, single
  byte, all-0xFF, all-0x00, max HID report size)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): project all recurring bill occurrences for Monthly Bills KPI (#135)

* feat(dashboard): add server-side aggregated dashboard endpoints

Add 6 GET endpoints under /api/v1/dashboard/ that replace 12+ individual
frontend data-fetching calls with focused server-side computation:

- GET /dashboard/kpis — liquid money, invested, interest, income, monthly expenses
- GET /dashboard/net-worth — monthly net worth history + 6-month projection
- GET /dashboard/portfolio — portfolio allocation (liquid vs invested)
- GET /dashboard/money-flow — Sankey diagram (nodes/links) + gained/lost summary
- GET /dashboard/monthly-expenses — bills by month/category with trend lines
- GET /dashboard/lists — active subscriptions and top merchants for current month

Each endpoint runs parallel DB queries and applies the same computation logic
previously done client-side (linear regression, toMonthlyAmount, DISTINCT ON
for latest balances, RLS enforced via app.client_id session variable).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(dashboard): split monolithic service into query files

- dashboard-service.ts is now a thin 46-line orchestrator
- Extracted each endpoint's logic to queries/:
  - queries/kpis.ts
  - queries/net-worth.ts
  - queries/portfolio.ts
  - queries/money-flow.ts
  - queries/monthly-expenses.ts
  - queries/lists.ts
- Shared helpers in dashboard-helpers.ts (toMonthlyAmount, normalizeCategory, currentMonthRange)
- Shared response types in dashboard-types.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(dashboard): address code review findings

- Remove duplicate then reassignment from test mock chain (noThenProperty)
- Rename abbreviated loop vars in monthly-expenses, net-worth, portfolio
  (b/cat/dateStr/amount/m/p/c/pt/apiCat -> descriptive names)
- Skipped: z.number() schema changes (services and interfaces use number correctly)
- Skipped: limit(500) time-window refactor (design decision, not a confirmed bug)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(dashboard): use descriptive variable names in net-worth and monthly-expenses queries

- Replace remaining abbreviated callback args (h, s, v, bc, cat) with descriptive names

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(dashboard): return money values as formatted strings

All monetary fields now use .toFixed(2) string representation:
- DashboardKpisResponse: liquidMoney, investedMoney, etc.
- NetWorthPoint: value, projection
- PortfolioItem: value
- LiquidFlowLink: value
- liquidFlowSummary: gained, lost, netChange
- ListItem: total; totalSubscriptions, totalReceipts

Update Zod schemas, types, query files, and tests accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(bills): add optional recurrence field for recurring bills

- Add recurrence column (varchar 16, nullable) to billsTable
- Migration: 0005_recurring_bills.sql
- Update UpsertBillRequestSchema and UpsertBillResponseSchema
- Update BillSummary interface to include recurrence
- Persist and return recurrence in all bills service methods
- Dashboard KPIs: project recurring bills not yet recorded this month
- Dashboard monthly-expenses: inject projected recurring bills into chart
- Helpers: nextExpectedDate and computeProjectedBillsAmount

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dashboard): project all recurring bill occurrences for Monthly Bills KPI (weekly/bi-weekly/monthly)

- Remove skip for categories with bills already in month
- Now sums all future occurrences for accurate projection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(dashboard): include projected recurring bills in money flow calculation

- Query latest recurring bills for projection
- Sum all future occurrences within current month
- Add projected amount to billsOut like Monthly Bills KPI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove remaining conflict markers from kpis.ts

* fix: resolve merge conflicts in monthly-expenses.ts

* fix: critical dashboard and bills issues

- Add 'bi-weekly' to BILL_RECURRENCES in bills table
- Add 'quarterly' case to toMonthlyAmount function
- Fix infinite loop risk in computeProjectedBillsAmount by:
  - Validating recurrence before processing
  - Passing date-only strings to nextExpectedDate
  - Detecting non-advancing dates to break safely
- Add recurrence field to updateData in bills-service updateBillByCategoryId

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
